projects.projectName: Central West Orana REZ Transmission Project (Stage 1)
projects.summary: Development of the Central West Orana Renewable Energy Zone's transmission infrastructure. This involves new 330kV transmission lines and associated substations, critical for connecting future large-scale renewable projects. Significant HV equipment procurement (transformers, circuit breakers, GIS) for ongoing construction stages and commissioning.

Planning and early development for the Hunter-Central Coast REZ transmission infrastructure, including new transmission lines and substations to unlock significant renewable energy capacity. Substantial future HV equipment needs (transformers, circuit breakers, GIS) with major procurement expected from late 2024.

A new 500kV interconnector linking Victoria and NSW, crucial for transferring renewable energy. Involves extensive new transmission lines and substations with high-voltage equipment, including HV transformers, circuit breakers, and GIS, with ongoing construction and procurement for specialist components.

A 1500 MW capacity HVDC interconnector between Tasmania and Victoria, consisting of two subsea cables and associated converter stations. Early works are underway, with major procurement for HVDC converter stations, HV transformers, and HV switchgear active or imminent in 2024.

The world's largest grid-forming battery, essential for NSW grid stability. While main construction is underway for commissioning in 2025, specialist sub-packages for HV switchyard connections, FAT for HV transformers and circuit breakers, and commissioning inspection remain active or imminent in 2024.

One of Australia's largest proposed solar and battery projects (up to 1.5GW solar, 2GWh BESS), requiring substantial new grid connection infrastructure and high-voltage equipment, including HV transformers and circuit breakers. Final Investment Decision expected late 2024 / early 2025.

One of the largest onshore wind farms in Australia, with ongoing construction and commissioning targeted for 2024/2025. This includes significant new transmission infrastructure to connect to the grid, requiring substantial specialist HV equipment (transformers, circuit breakers) FAT and inspection.

A multi-project renewable energy hub with over 1GW proposed capacity, including solar and BESS. These projects will require new grid connections and substation infrastructure with high-voltage transformers and circuit breakers. FID for various components expected late 2024.

Expansion of an existing large-scale solar farm to add 200MW capacity and potentially a BESS. This will involve new solar arrays, associated grid connection upgrades, and procurement of HV equipment (transformers, switchgear) with FID expected late 2024 / early 2025.

projects.projectName: New England Solar Farm Stage 2 & BESS
projects.summary: Expansion of a major solar farm to include additional capacity (400MW total) and a large battery storage system (200MW/400MWh). Construction for Stage 2 and BESS is advancing, implying active specialist procurement, FAT, and commissioning for HV grid connection infrastructure.

Proposed large-scale battery energy storage system (200MW/400MWh) to be co-located with the Limondale Solar Farm. It requires new HV grid connection infrastructure, including HV transformers and circuit breakers. FID expected late 2024 / early 2025.

Ambitious multi-technology renewable hub integrating wind, solar, storage, and hydrogen production, targeting up to 3.5GW. Requires extensive new HV grid infrastructure (transmission lines, substations, transformers, circuit breakers). Early stages with FID targeted for 2025+.

Integrated renewable energy hub including wind, solar, BESS and green hydrogen production. Initial phases are underway, with hydrogen plant in early stages. This requires significant HV infrastructure for generation and electrolysis, implying ongoing procurement of transformers and switchgear.

projects.projectName: AirTrunk SYD2 expansion / New Hyperscale Data Centre Projects
projects.summary: AirTrunk, a major hyperscale data centre operator, is continually expanding its facilities in Australia (e.g., SYD2 expansion, new campuses). These projects involve significant power infrastructure upgrades, new dedicated substations, and high-voltage grid connections, requiring ongoing FAT and inspection for HV transformers and circuit breakers.

Digital Realty is expanding its data centre footprint in Australia (e.g., DRT2 Sydney). These expansions require substantial upgrades to grid connections and new dedicated substation infrastructure to support increasing power demands, involving ongoing procurement and inspection of HV transformers and circuit breakers.

projects.epcContractor: McConnell Dowell & John Holland Joint Venture
projects.summary: A pioneering pumped hydro energy storage project, converting an old gold mine. Construction targets 2025/2026 completion. Involves significant new HV transmission lines and substation infrastructure for grid connection, requiring extensive ongoing FAT and commissioning inspection for HV equipment (transformers, circuit breakers).

Major redevelopment of existing hydro power stations to increase capacity and flexibility, including significant upgrades to generation and transmission assets. This involves ongoing specialist sub-package procurement for new HV equipment (transformers, circuit breakers, GIS) targeting completion by 2028.

Proposed solar farm (110MW) with potential battery storage (50MW/100MWh), requiring new HV substation and grid connection infrastructure, including HV transformers and circuit breakers. FID expected late 2024 / early 2025.

Ambitious green hydrogen project powered by solar (1.5GW) and battery storage (400MW/1GWh BESS) with 1GW electrolyser. Requires massive HV grid connection and substation infrastructure for electrolysis and export, including numerous HV transformers and circuit breakers. FID anticipated late 2024 / 2025.

Proposed 190MW wind farm in the Central West Orana REZ, for which EPC procurement is currently underway. This implies major HV equipment procurement for new substation and grid connection (transformers, circuit breakers) is imminent or active.
Proposed 240MW wind farm in South Australia requiring new HV grid connection and substation infrastructure, including HV transformers and circuit breakers. FID expected late 2024 / 2025.

Large hybrid renewable energy project (1.2 GW wind, solar, BESS) requiring extensive new HV grid infrastructure, including new substations, HV transmission lines, transformers, and circuit breakers. Approvals progressing, FID targeted late 2024 / 2025.

A massive proposed renewable energy and green hydrogen production complex (up to 2.5GW) in Western Victoria, requiring substantial new HV transmission and substation infrastructure, including numerous HV transformers and circuit breakers. Early stage planning with long lead times for major HV procurement.

projects.projectName: White Rock Wind Farm Stage 2 & BESS
projects.summary: Expansion of an existing wind farm to include additional turbines (100MW) and potential battery storage, necessitating upgrades or new HV grid connection infrastructure. This will involve procurement of HV transformers and circuit breakers, with FID expected late 2024 / early 2025.

projects.projectName: Southern Downs Renewable Energy Zone Transmission
projects.summary: Planning for new transmission infrastructure to enable the Southern Downs Renewable Energy Zone, involving significant HV line and substation development. This will require procurement of HV transformers, circuit breakers, and associated equipment from late 2024 onwards.

Proposed large-scale wind farm (250MW) coupled with battery storage (200MW/400MWh), requiring new HV substation and grid connection infrastructure, including HV transformers and circuit breakers. FID expected late 2024 / early 2025.

projects.projectName: Goyder South Stage 2 Wind Farm & BESS
projects.summary: Significant expansion of the Goyder South renewable energy complex, adding substantial wind (412MW) and battery storage (900MW BESS) capacity. This necessitates additional HV grid connection infrastructure, including HV transformers and circuit breakers. FID expected late 2024 / early 2025.

projects.projectName: Western Victoria REZ Transmission (Western Renewables Link)
projects.summary: Development of major transmission lines to connect new renewable energy projects in Western Victoria to the grid. Early works and land acquisition are progressing, with ongoing major construction for HV substations and lines, requiring continuous procurement and inspection of HV transformers, circuit breakers, and GIS.

A new 900km high-voltage transmission line linking NSW and SA, including new substations and upgrades. Major works are ongoing, targeting 2024/2026 completion. Specialist FAT, QAQC surveillance, and commissioning inspection for HV equipment (transformers, circuit breakers, GIS) will be active throughout 2024.

Large-scale battery energy storage system (150MW/150MWh) at a former thermal power station site. Construction is ongoing, targeting 2024 completion. Specialist FAT, QAQC surveillance, and commissioning inspection for new HV substation components (transformers, circuit breakers) remain active or imminent.

Developing a green hydrogen production facility, initially with a 1MW electrolyser, but planning for much larger scale. Early stages of the electrolyser plant are under construction, requiring ongoing HV grid connection work and associated HV equipment procurement and inspection (transformers, circuit breakers).

A large integrated renewable energy development in the New England REZ, including wind, solar, and BESS, targeting over 1.2GW. Requires substantial new HV grid infrastructure (transmission lines, substations, transformers, circuit breakers). Various components are in planning, with FID targeted for 2025+.

Part of ANEEL's December 2023 transmission auction, Lot 1 involves construction of new transmission lines and substations, including significant HV equipment, in multiple states. EPC and equipment procurement (transformers, circuit breakers, GIS) are active or imminent in 2024.

Part of ANEEL's December 2023 transmission auction, Lot 6 includes extensive new HV transmission lines and substations, requiring substantial HV equipment procurement (transformers, circuit breakers, GIS) active or imminent in 2024.

projects.owner: State Government of Ceará, numerous private companies
projects.summary: A major hub for green hydrogen production, attracting multiple large-scale projects (e.g., Fortescue, EDP, AES Brasil) with pilot projects operating and larger scale advancing. These require significant new renewable energy generation and associated HV grid infrastructure (substations, HV transformers, circuit breakers) for electrolysers and port facilities, with continuous procurement.

projects.projectName: Santo Antônio do Leite Wind Complex
projects.summary: Large-scale wind farm project with 662.4 MW capacity, which received BNDES financing in 2023 and is currently under construction. This implies ongoing specialist procurement for HV components (transformers, circuit breakers) and commissioning activities through 2024.

One of Latin America's largest solar complexes, with over 1.2 GW planned capacity. While parts are operational, expansion phases are ongoing, meaning continuous procurement for new solar farms and associated HV grid infrastructure (transformers, circuit breakers) is active through 2024.

Large wind farm (310MW) in a key wind region. Recently secured BNDES financing (late 2023) indicates it's in an active construction phase where specialist HV packages (transformers, circuit breakers) for grid connection are open or imminent for procurement and inspection in 2024.

Ongoing construction of a solar complex (approx. 200MW), with Phase 1 operational and Phase 2 in construction. This implies continuous procurement for new solar farms and associated HV grid infrastructure (transformers, circuit breakers) active through 2024.

One of Latin America's largest wind farms (846MW), with construction nearing completion but still within the 2024 window for specialist FAT, QAQC surveillance, and commissioning inspections for HV equipment (transformers, circuit breakers).

projects.projectName: Rio do Vento Wind and Solar Complex - Expansion
projects.location: Rio do Vento, Rio Grande do Norte
projects.summary: Large existing complex with continuous expansion for new wind, solar (1GW+) and BESS capacity. This implies ongoing HV equipment procurement and inspection needs (transformers, circuit breakers) for new phases being integrated in 2024.

projects.location: São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, Fortaleza, Vinhedo
projects.summary: Ascenty is a major hyperscale data centre provider in Brazil, continually building new facilities and expanding existing ones across multiple states. This involves significant investment in new dedicated substations and high-voltage grid connections, requiring ongoing FAT and inspection for HV transformers and circuit breakers.

projects.location: São Paulo, Campinas, Porto Alegre, Fortaleza
projects.summary: Scala is rapidly expanding its hyperscale data centre operations across Latin America, with significant investment in Brazil. This includes the construction of new dedicated substations and high-voltage grid connections for their multi-megawatt facilities, requiring ongoing FAT and inspection for HV transformers and circuit breakers.

projects.projectName: Porto de Suape Green Hydrogen Hub
projects.owner: State Government of Pernambuco, various private companies
projects.summary: Another significant green hydrogen hub development, attracting multiple renewable energy and electrolyser projects that will require substantial new HV grid infrastructure (substations, HV transformers, circuit breakers). MOU signed, feasibility studies ongoing, projects moving to FID in 2024/2025.

Large-scale solar PV plant (437 MWac) under construction, with commercial operation planned for 2024. This implies ongoing FAT, QAQC surveillance, and commissioning inspection for HV equipment (transformers, circuit breakers) for its grid connection.

One of Brazil's largest solar projects (900 MWp), under construction with completion expected in 2024. Ongoing activities will include specialist HV equipment (transformers, circuit breakers) FAT, QAQC surveillance, and commissioning inspections for its grid connection.

Large-scale solar project (200 MWac) currently under construction with operations expected in the second half of 2024. This implies active HV equipment (transformers, circuit breakers) procurement and inspection needs for its grid connection.

Large wind complex (660 MW) nearing completion, with phases energizing in late 2023 and early 2024. Still relevant for FAT, QAQC surveillance, and commissioning inspection for HV equipment (transformers, circuit breakers) related to grid connection.

projects.owner: To be determined by auction winners
projects.summary: ANEEL regularly schedules new transmission auctions, which are key drivers for major HV equipment procurement. These future auctions will define significant new transmission lines and substations, requiring procurement of HV transformers, circuit breakers, and GIS from late 2024 / 2025 onwards.

Expansion of the Campo Largo wind complex, with new phase (117 MW) under construction targeting H2 2024 operation. This indicates ongoing HV equipment (transformers, circuit breakers) inspection and commissioning needs for its grid connection.

New large-scale solar project (300 MWac) under construction, targeting H2 2024 operation. This requires ongoing FAT and commissioning inspection for HV equipment (transformers, circuit breakers) related to its new grid connection.

Omega Energia operates large wind complexes and is continuously expanding or adding storage. New phases for the Parnaíba II complex are in planning, necessitating ongoing HV equipment procurement and inspection (transformers, circuit breakers) from late 2024 onwards.
